verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted glycoprotein (signal peptide, no TM domain, no GPI anchor) produced by epididymal epithelium. It is deposited onto the outer surface of sperm during epididymal transit. At least one molecular form (protein E) is stably and wash-resistantly bound to the sperm surface, persisting through acrosome reaction and migrating to the equatorial segment. This outer-leaflet surface association is exclusively in the germline/reproductive lineage (sperm cells), qualifying as tissue_restricted_surface. Outside this narrow reproductive compartment, CRISP1 is a secreted luminal protein with no stable surface anchoring.
